授業目的: This course is a continuation of our exploration of cross‑cultural communication. It deepens students’ understanding of English pragmatics by examining communication styles such as direct versus indirect approaches and politeness strategies across cultures. Students will investigate authentic texts, academic articles, and case studies to understand how language shapes and is shaped by cultural expectations. Throughout the semester, learners will conduct a research project and report the findings of their research, integrating theoretical insights with practical observations.
到達目標: By the end of the course, students will be able to:1. Analyze English Pragmatics: Explain key pragmatic concepts such as speech acts, implicature, and context, and evaluate their role in intercultural communication.2. Compare Communication Styles: Identify and compare direct and indirect communication approaches, discussing how these strategies are employed in Japanese, Western, and other cultural contexts.3. Evaluate Politeness Strategies: Critically examine theories of politeness and assess how these strategies function in various communication scenarios.4. Integrate Theory and Practice: Combine insights from pragmatic theory with real-world case studies to deepen understanding of cultural communication dynamics.5. Conduct and Report Research: Design and execute a research project on topics related to English pragmatics and cultural communication, and effectively report the findings of that research.
授業方法: Classes are conducted face-to-face in the classroom.

第1回:
【Startup Week】Course Introduction & Overview• Review of cross‑cultural communication• Introduction to English pragmatics and cultural styles
(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第2回: 【Startup Week】Group Discussion. In this session, you will share your ideas about how culture affects communication.  (Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第3回: 【Core Weeks①】Basics of English Pragmatics. You will learn key ideas such as speech acts and hints in conversation, and why context is important. (Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第4回: 【Core Weeks②】You will talk about examples of pragmatics in daily life. (Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第5回: 【Core Weeks③】Direct vs. Indirect Communication. You will learn what direct and indirect communication are and see examples from different cultures. (Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第6回: 【Core Weeks④】You will compare and contrast direct and indirect communication, discussing the benefits and challenges of each style. Homework: Share your examples and opinions during the discussion.(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第7回: 【Core Weeks⑤】Politeness Theories. You will review simple ideas about politeness, including basic theories such as those by Brown and Levinson.(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第8回: 【Core Weeks⑥】Group Discussion. You will talk about how politeness works in different settings, such as at school or in everyday life.(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第9回: 【Core Weeks⑦】Combining Pragmatics and Culture. You will examine a case study that shows how culture affects communication, including problems and successes.(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第10回:
【Core Weeks⑧】You will share your research ideas and discuss different methods with your peers. Homework: Bring your research question ideas to the discussion.
(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第11回: 【Core Weeks⑨】Research Methods for Pragmatics & Culture. You will learn simple research methods used in language studies and begin to think about your own research questions. (Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第12回: 【Core Weeks⑩】You will share your research ideas and discuss different methods with your peers(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第13回: 【Core Weeks⑪】Planning Your Research. You will start planning your research project by designing your research framework and outlining your project.(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第14回: 【Core Weeks⑫】Group Discussion. You will discuss your research plans and get feedback from your classmates.(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第15回: 【Core Weeks⑬】Digital Communication and Social Media. You will learn how digital tools and social media influence cross‑cultural communication.(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第16回: 【Core Weeks⑭】Discuss the role of digital media in communication and share your ideas about its impact on cultural interactions.(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第17回: 【Core Weeks⑮】Communication in Professional and Academic Settings. You will study how communication works in global business and educational environments, including challenges and best practices.(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第18回: 【Core Weeks⑯】WRITING - Thesis Statement, Body Paragraph 1 + 2 + 3 + introduction + conclusion(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第27回: 【Flex Hours】Online class (On-demand format and/or Task-oriented format)《90minutes》
第28回: 【Flex Hours】Online class (On-demand format and/or Task-oriented format)《90minutes》
第29回: 【Classes and Examination Period】Course Wrap‑Up & Reflection:• Reflect on key learnings, challenges, and successes(Face-to-face class)《90minutes 》
第30回: 【Classes and Examination Period】Final Examination(Face-to-face class)《90minutes》
トピック.授業計画1:
Lessons held in the computer lab will be focused on research and writing for the final essay and presentation. Lessons held in normal classrooms will be for lectures, group discussions, and textbook activities.
Skills that will be highlighted in this course:
Writing an argumentative/persuasive essay that argues for one side of a global issue;
synthesizing information; quoting, paraphrasing, and summarizing, supporting opinions with facts and statistics, critical thinking, research skills, basic APA citation format, avoiding plagiarism, gathering data through field research
トピック.授業外学習2: Students must be highly motivated to succeed in this course. They will need to research, read, and prepare discussions for authentic English texts outside of class. Students must also work on their own research papers for homework.

学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic:
各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。
DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する以下の能力が養成されます。
言語教育科目：１．言語コミュニケーション能力：外国語力を生かし、積極的に発信できる。２．異文化理解・多文化共生力：異文化に対する感受性をもち、多様な文化と共生できる。
